commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Protected maintainer labeling plus the new QuickJS host bridge make the remaining action a human owner/security decision, not an automated repair.; Needs attention: The diff intentionally adds a new QuickJS guest-to-host namespace bridge and changes shrinkwrap state, so maintainer security review is still required even though I found no concrete exploit. (sha=eac4ab377ee46b275fb8d9069406f321afa01d72)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds internal code-mode namespace registration and QuickJS bridging, updates code-mode docs/tests, adds live repro scripts, clears namespaces on plugin rollback, and removes a stale root `tokenjuice` shrinkwrap entry.

PR surface: Source +547, Tests +497, Docs +44, Other +688. Total +1776 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. The PR body provides live model runs, remote Crabbox run IDs, Docker repro evidence, and observed namespace gating behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer labeling plus the new QuickJS host bridge make the remaining action a human owner/security decision, not an automated repair.

target.title: feat: add internal code mode namespaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Add an internal code-mode namespace registry and QuickJS bridge so host-owned namespace globals can be exposed inside `exec` without adding model-visible tools.
- Gate namespaces by run catalog ownership, serialize only safe enumerable scope values, reject forged/unsafe paths, preserve namespace runtime across `wait`, and clear namespace registrations on plugin rollback.
- Document the internal namespace boundary and explicitly keep a public plugin SDK namespace API out of this PR until it can be loader-owned and cache-safe.
- Add a live OpenAI benchmark harness for a fake Fictions service, plus a standalone live Docker repro script for the namespace mode.
- Tighten the exec prompt so models know namespace calls must `return` a final value.

## Verification

- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (44 passed)
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/api-baseline.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 passed)
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `env OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=0 fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm check:changed`
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm build`
- `node --import tsx scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts --tasks=3` (regular 3/3, code-namespace 3/3)
- `pnpm crabbox:run -- --allow-env OPENAI_API_KEY --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --shell -- 'node --import tsx scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts --tasks=3'` (lease `cbx_6a216056aab1`, run `run_909ea916a62f`, regular 3/3, code-namespace 3/3)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` (90 passed)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/code-mode.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts`
- `bash -n scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live-docker.sh scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live-scenario.sh`
- `node --check scripts/check-docker-e2e-boundaries.mjs && node --check scripts/lib/docker-e2e-scenarios.mjs && node scripts/check-docker-e2e-boundaries.mjs`
- `CI=1 node scripts/check-docker-e2e-boundaries.mjs && CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/docker-e2e-plan.test.ts` (remote run `run_32d32d42fb03`, 26 passed)
- `OPENCLAW_CODE_MODE_LIVE_TASKS=1 node --import tsx scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts` (regular 1/1, code-namespace 1/1)
- `OPENCLAW_CODE_MODE_LIVE_TASKS=3 OPENCLAW_DOCKER_BUILD_ON_MISSING=1 pnpm test:docker:live-code-mode` (remote run `run_f5633d35f8e0`, regular 3/3, code-namespace 3/3; this was before moving the Docker wrapper out of `scripts/e2e`)
- `OPENCLAW_CODE_MODE_LIVE_TASKS=1 OPENCLAW_DOCKER_BUILD_ON_MISSING=1 pnpm test:docker:live-code-mode` (remote run `run_4ae68a8c9b13`, regular 1/1, code-namespace 1/1; the temporary npm alias executed `bash scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live-docker.sh` and was removed afterward to keep dependency guard out of this PR)
- `CI=1 node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts && CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` (remote run `run_3e82078ddc9d`, Madge 0 cycles, 90 passed)
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:check` (after rebase onto current `origin/main`)
- `pnpm check:test-types` (after rebase onto current `origin/main`)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.wizard.config.ts --reporter=verbose` (87 passed)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/code-mode.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts src/agents/code-mode-namespaces.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/wizard/setup.official-plugins.test.ts scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts`
- `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts` (0 cycles)
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm check:test-types` (after final rebase)
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm tsgo:extensions && node scripts/check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs --mode=compile` (110 plugin package-boundary compiles passed)
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings after final rebase cleanup)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: code mode can expose internal host-owned namespace globals inside QuickJS while keeping normal tools hidden behind `exec` and `wait`; live model runs can use that namespace surface to inspect and mutate a service. The Docker script is an explicit source-level live repro, not a cataloged release/package Docker E2E lane.

Real environment tested: local macOS checkout with Node 24.15.0; AWS c7a.8xlarge remote runs `run_909ea916a62f`, `run_f5633d35f8e0`, `run_4ae68a8c9b13`, `run_3e82078ddc9d`; OpenAI Responses API with `gpt-5.4-mini` and a real `OPENAI_API_KEY` passed through remote env forwarding.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: targeted code-mode Vitest, plugin SDK API baseline test, plugin SDK API check, local changed gate and build from the original namespace patch; after the live harness addition, ran local and remote Fictions benchmarks, focused code-mode Vitest, targeted oxlint, Docker wrapper syntax/catalog guards, remote Docker repro, Madge cycle proof, whitespace check, and final autoreview as listed above.

Evidence after fix: local live benchmark passed 6/6 task-mode pairs: regular 3/3 with 10,758 ms, 9 model turns, 7 top-level tool calls, 7 service calls; code-namespace 3/3 with 17,248 ms, 9 model turns, 6 top-level tool calls, 4 service calls. Remote live benchmark passed 6/6 task-mode pairs: regular 3/3 with 12,228 ms, 9 model turns, 7 top-level tool calls, 7 service calls; code-namespace 3/3 with 7,728 ms, 9 model turns, 6 top-level tool calls, 4 service calls. The post-move live Docker smoke passed 2/2 task-mode pairs: regular 1/1 with 2,815 ms and 3 service calls; code-namespace 1/1 with 2,287 ms and 1 service call.

Observed result after fix: namespace globals are visible only when the owning plugin id has the required catalog tools in the run, same-named tools from other owners do not expose the namespace, forged namespace paths are rejected, namespace calls survive suspend/resume, and the live Fictions namespace completed risk audit, status promotion, and invoice aggregation tasks with fewer service calls than regular mode.

What was not tested: no real third-party plugin namespace integration, because this PR intentionally does not add a public plugin SDK namespace API. A direct post-alias remote retry of `bash scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live-docker.sh` lost SSH during package build before the wrapper reached the test; the same wrapper path had already passed in `run_4ae68a8c9b13`. A repeat broad `pnpm check:changed` after the live harness commit did not complete because local `tsgolint` crashed with a Go SIGSEGV and a Blacksmith Testbox rerun stalled on its `Run Testbox` step; targeted lint/test/live proof passed after that.

commands.author: keshavbotagent

target.title: fix(codex): prevent false completion stalls during native streams
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep raw assistant progress on the documented `turn/completed` terminal guard; progress text is still not treated as a final answer.
- Count forwarded native Codex response stream deltas, including `response.custom_tool_call_input.delta`, as active turn activity and attempt progress while a turn is already open.
- Guard unscoped native `response.*.delta` notifications so they only refresh a turn when the shared app-server client has one active lease; concurrent leased turns no longer keep each other's watchdogs alive.
- Keep the post-tool raw-assistant fallback timeout conservative at `5 * 60_000` / `300000` ms for app-server/native paths that do not forward stream deltas, and align runtime docs plus plugin metadata.
- Preserve recovery behavior for actual completion stalls: replay only replay-safe attempts; for side-effecting/non-replay-safe stalls retire the stuck app-server client, clear stale native thread binding only when it still points at the timed-out thread, release the lane, and surface a recoverable incomplete-turn result.
- After the one replay-safe stdio retry is exhausted, a repeated `turn_completion_idle_timeout` now falls through to the normal timeout payload path instead of rejecting the embedded run.

## Why this shape
The failure is not that raw assistant progress needs to become deliverable final text. The failure is that native Codex can spend a long time actively streaming a large tool input/patch after a raw assistant progress item has completed, before any completed tool-call notification exists. Without counting those `response.*.delta` events as activity, OpenClaw can falsely fire `turn.completion_idle_timeout` while the model is still working.

This keeps the lifecycle semantics strict: final delivery still requires `turn/completed`; active native stream deltas only keep the watchdog from killing a live turn. The latest review fixes also avoid cross-talk for unscoped deltas and keep retry-exhausted completion-idle failures on the timeout-result path.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: Codex-backed Telegram turns could be falsely aborted while native Codex was still streaming response deltas for a large tool input/patch after raw assistant progress, producing `Codex stopped before confirming the turn was complete. Some work may already have been performed; verify the current state before retrying.`
- Real environment tested: Keshav's live OpenClaw gateway and Telegram group flow on 2026-05-29 IST.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Keshav built and restarted the gateway from this PR branch after the native-delta runtime fix, then reattempted the Vietnam webpage edit flow that had repeatedly failed with the completion-idle watchdog. The latest pushed head `2aa61b24ea` is rebased onto `origin/main` `040eba1cdc`; the final commit on top is test-only stabilization after the runtime proof.
- Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the Telegram operator thread:

```text
2026-05-29 21:46 IST, Keshav G:
I built and restarted server
Then reattempted the vietnam webpage and yay it worked this time without errors

2026-05-29 23:41 IST, Keshav G:
I have build and restarted
Now do the tests, ci and submit latest proof from latest Heads
```

- Observed result after fix: The previously repeated Vietnam webpage edit flow completed without the prior `turn.completion_idle_timeout` / `Codex stopped before confirming the turn was complete` error after the gateway was built and restarted from the PR branch.
- What was not tested: The bot did not personally restart the live gateway; Keshav performed the build/restart. Latest GitHub Actions were still running when this proof was added, and local validation is listed below.

## Review feedback addressed
Latest head: `2aa61b24ea` on `origin/main` base `040eba1cdc`.

- Plugin config metadata and both Codex harness docs now agree with the runtime default: `postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` defaults to `300000` ms.
- Timeout cleanup now clears the persisted Codex app-server binding only if the stored binding still points at the timed-out thread, preserving transient-turn bindings.
- Unscoped native response deltas now count as activity only when the shared app-server client reports a single active lease; scoped deltas for the active turn still count directly.
- Retry-exhausted replay-safe stdio `turn_completion_idle_timeout` failures now surface the timeout payload instead of throwing the prompt error after the one retry has already been used.
- Added focused regression coverage for binding-preserving cleanup, concurrent-lease unscoped deltas, native response delta activity, and retry-exhausted completion-idle timeout handling.

## Tests / validation
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed on head `2aa61b24ea`.
- Targeted `oxfmt --check` passed on changed TypeScript/plugin files on head `2aa61b24ea`.
- Focused Vitest passed on head `2aa61b24ea`: `6` files / `82` tests.

Focused Vitest command:

```bash
./node_modules/.bin/vitest run \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-results.test.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-timeouts.test.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts \
  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.codex-app-server-recovery.test.ts \
  --reporter=dot
```

## Related issue
- Related to #87744. This PR addresses the Codex-backed Telegram completion-idle family; the live proof narrows one concrete false-timeout cause to native response delta streams not being counted as turn activity.
target.author: keshavbotagent
